input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows an engineering drawing of the vehicle on the top accompanied by six CAD views of the vehicle on the bottom. The six CAD views each feature a different orientation of our design, so that 3D information about our design can be inferred. In the CAD views, the main hoop is highlighted in pink and the main hoop braces are highlighted in blue. The CAD views are provided to contextualize the engineering drawing, which has the same orientation as one of the six CAD views. All units displayed in the engineering drawing have units of mm. Based on the engineering drawing, does our design comply with rule F.5.9.4 specified in the FSAE rule document? Only use dimensions explicitly shown in the engineering drawing to answer the question. If a dimension is not explicitly shown, you can assume that it complies with the rules. First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with 'Explanation:'). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with 'Answer:') that summarizes your response.

retrieved_context:
RULE: f.5.9.4
            TITLE: the main hoop braces must be attached 160 mm or less below the top most surface of the

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.9.4: The Main Hoop Braces must be attached 160 mm or less below the top most surface of the
F.5.9.4 The Main Hoop Braces must be attached 160 mm or less below the top most surface of the 
Main Hoop.   
The Main Hoop Braces should be attached as near as possible to the top of the Main Hoop

intermediate_steps.yolo_config_raw:
{
    "detection_model": "YOLOv26",
    "slice_height": 4000,

    "overlap_height_ratio": 0.9,
    "overlap_width_ratio": 0.9,
    "keywords": [
        "dimension line",
        "dimension arrow",
        "numerical value",
        "main hoop",
        "main hoop braces"
    ]
}
intermediate_steps.founded_objects: main hoop, dimension line, dimension arrow, numerical value, main hoop braces

final_answer:
Explanation: Rule F.5.9.4 requires that the main hoop braces must be attached 160 mm or less below the top most surface of the Main Hoop. The engineering drawing explicitly shows a vertical dimension of 161.06 mm between the top of the main hoop and the level of the main hoop braces. Since 161.06 mm is greater than the maximum allowed dimension of 160 mm, the design does not comply with this rule.
Answer: No
